markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Kuala Lumpur be 35°C on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/my/sepang-district/WMKK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket closely splits between 33°C (31.5% implied probability) and 34°C (31.5%), reflecting divergent short-range forecasts amid Kuala Lumpur's typical May climatology of average highs near 33°C. MetMalaysia's latest advisory projects a 35°C maximum with isolated afternoon thunderstorms following clear mornings, potentially allowing peak heating before convective suppression via clouds and outflows. Global models, as aggregated in sites like weather.com, imply ~34°C with 54% precipitation odds, tempering upside due to urban heat island effects at the Subang measurement station. Key differentiators include thunderstorm timing—earlier onset favors 33°C—and overnight model updates from ECMWF/GFS, which could shift sentiment before resolution on official observations.

markets.question: Incheon United FC vs. Gangwon FC: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming K-League game between Incheon United FC and Gangwon FC, scheduled for May 2 at 3:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Incheon United FC and Gangwon FC combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on kleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Incheon United FC vs. Gangwon FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the K-League game, scheduled for May 2 at 3:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incheon United, languishing in 12th place in K League 1 with just 4 points from 5 matches (1 win, 1 draw, 3 losses, 6-9 goal difference), host 6th-placed Gangwon FC (6 points, 6-6 goals) on May 2 at Sungui Arena Park. Incheon's recent form shows vulnerability, particularly at home with back-to-back losses to FC Seoul and Daejeon Hana Citizen, though a 1-0 away win over FC Anyang on March 22 offers slight momentum. Gangwon sits mid-table on balanced goal difference but inconsistent results, including a recent 0-1 loss to Pohang Steelers. No major injuries or suspensions reported; competitive head-to-head history (Gangwon 18 wins to Incheon's 14) and both teams' struggles for consistency shape trader consensus for a tight contest amid Incheon's tough upcoming fixtures against Gimcheon Sangmu and Ulsan HD FC.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no in-person meeting between President Trump and President Putin by June 30 (85%), reflecting the absence of any announced bilateral summit amid ongoing Ukraine ceasefire diplomacy conducted via phone calls rather than face-to-face talks. Reports of a U.S. invitation for Putin to the December G20 in Miami point to potential later engagement on U.S. soil, yet fall outside the market window. Neutral sites like China draw minor odds (5%) as speculative third-party venues, while logistical hurdles, Ukraine war escalation risks, and alliance pressures keep probabilities low across locations.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Gimhae Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/busan/RKPK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

markets.question: Will South Korea ETF (EWY) hit (LOW) $147 Week of April 27 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of April 27 2026, any 1-minute candle for South Korea ETF (EWY) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the South Korea ETF (EWY) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia Rodrigo's "Drop Dead" debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 dated May 2, 2026, marking her fourth career leader and propelled by explosive streaming, sales, and radio airplay as the lead single from her upcoming album. This fresh chart-topper underscores her sustained pop dominance, while Ella Langley's "Choosin' Texas" lingers at No. 2 after a record seven weeks at No. 1 this year, highlighting country crossover momentum. Eight artists have already claimed 2026 Hot 100 crowns, including Taylor Swift (twice), Bruno Mars, Harry Styles, Bad Bunny, BTS, and first-timer Ella Langley. With summer releases looming, trader focus shifts to heavyweights like Drake and Ariana Grande, whose promotional campaigns and viral TikTok traction could yield late-year No. 1s amid weekly chart battles driven by Luminate-tracked metrics. Next Hot 100 drops May 9, with risers like Olivia Dean's "Man I Need" posing early threats.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Manila be 38°C on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Ninoy Aquino International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Ninoy Aquino International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ph/manila/RPLL.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Urban heat island effects and low rain chances (10%) bolster potential for 37°C (31%) if solar insolation intensifies, though model consensus shows limited cooling risks; daily PAGASA updates and real-time Wunderground data could refine these odds as conditions evolve under neutral ENSO influences.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rep. Kevin Hern's 90.5% implied probability in the Oklahoma Republican Senate primary reflects trader consensus on his commanding position to succeed Sen. Markwayne Mullin, whose March nomination as DHS Secretary opened the Class II seat. Trump's swift endorsement after Hern's March 11 announcement prompted Gov. Kevin Stitt and Rep. Stephanie Bice to withdraw, bolstering Hern's fundraising lead of over $2 million in cash on hand and securing nods from Senate Majority Leader John Thune, Sen. Tim Scott, and Club for Growth. The April filing deadline produced a fragmented field of lesser-known challengers like Wayne Lonny Washington. With no major developments in the past 30 days, odds remain firm ahead of the June 16 primary, though a scandal, legal challenge, or late health issue could disrupt Hern's path.

markets.question: Will Hunter Brown strike out the most batters during the 2026 MLB regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the pitcher who records the most strikeouts among pitchers during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the pitcher that records fewer innings pitched during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that records the lower ERA during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the pitcher that walked fewer batters during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the pitcher whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Houlahan faces no Democratic primary challengers after a rival withdrew, while Republican Marty Young remains the sole GOP filer without notable traction or national attention. This stability persists absent recent catalysts, with low trading volume underscoring sustained conviction. Potential shifts could arise from a stronger GOP nominee emerging post-primary, a Houlahan scandal or health issue, or a national Republican midterm wave overcoming the seat's entrenched Democratic path to victory.

markets.question: Will Drake feature Lil Durk on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed artists who feature on Drake's album "ICEMAN".

To qualify as "featured", the listed artist must be credited on at least one song on the album according to at least one major streaming platform: namely Spotify, Apple Music, Amazon Music, or YouTube Music.

If the album fails to release by December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source of this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tarik Skubal holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the 2026 AL Cy Young at 20.5% implied probability, but the race remains tightly contested early in the season due to breakout performances from young arms like Parker Messick (1.73 ERA, 38 strikeouts in 36.1 innings for Cleveland) and Cam Schlittler (0.74 ERA leading the AL for the Yankees), who earned third in MLB's first Cy Young poll two days ago. Jose Soriano surges with a majors-leading ERA near 0.86 across 42 innings and tied for most strikeouts at 49, while Dylan Cease rounds out the top five. Skubal's solid 2.70 ERA and 45 strikeouts are tempered by a dip in fastball velocity and strikeout rate, keeping the wisdom of crowds spread across these high-upside starters with sub-3.00 ERAs and strong WHIPs amid minimal innings thresholds met.

markets.question: Real Salt Lake vs. Portland Timbers: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLS game between Real Salt Lake and Portland Timbers, scheduled for May 2 at 4:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Real Salt Lake and Portland Timbers combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on mlssoccer.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Real Salt Lake vs. Portland Timbers - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for May 2 at 4:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Real Salt Lake enters the May 2 home matchup against Portland Timbers riding a five-match unbeaten streak in MLS, bolstered by a 3-1 win over Sporting Kansas City where Diego Luna scored in his first start post-knee injury, contributing to their strong 4-1-1 start and top-six Western Conference position. Portland Timbers, mired in poor form at 1-1-3 overall and lower-table standing, conceded late for a 3-2 loss at Vancouver Whitecaps despite Juan Mosquera's golazo and assist, amid ongoing injury concerns for defender Zac McGraw (back) and forward O. Fernandez (foot). RSL's home dominance at America First Field and head-to-head parity heighten trader focus on form disparity and roster health ahead of this Western Conference clash.

markets.question: Shymkent 2: Edas Butvilas vs Max Hans Rehberg
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Edas Butvilas and Max Hans Rehberg in the Shymkent 2, originally scheduled for May 1, 2026 at 2:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Edas Butvilas' if Edas Butvilas advances against Max Hans Rehberg.

This market will resolve to 'Max Hans Rehberg' if Max Hans Rehberg advances against Edas Butvilas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Facing him is qualifier Max Hans Rehberg (No. 641), who powered through qualifying before dominating Sergey Fomin 6-2, 6-0 in the second round—his third QF of 2026—riding momentum from recent upsets against higher-ranked foes. With no head-to-head history, trader consensus likely weighs Butvilas's superior ranking, experience, and direct entry against Rehberg's hot streak and clay adaptability, though upsets remain common in Challengers amid variable outdoor conditions.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's official observations confirm the highest air temperature on April 30, 2026, reached 26.7°C at the Observatory headquarters, solidifying trader consensus at 100% implied probability for "25°C or higher" in this market, which resolves based on that precise measurement. This outcome reflects an exceptionally warm April overall, driven by an anticyclone aloft delivering abundant sunshine and elevated daytime highs above seasonal norms of around 27–28°C. Early morning readings hovered near 21–22°C before rising under clear skies, consistent with regional subtropical climatology. With data now finalized via standard instrumentation and quality controls, realistic challenges are negligible—only a rare official revision from HKO could alter resolution, as seen in fewer than 0.1% of historical records.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,000 (HIGH) in June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for June 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

events.title: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of June?
events.description: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of June 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: The S&P 500 recently closed above 7,200 for the first time as of late April 2026, propelled by strong Q1 earnings growth exceeding 14% year-over-year, AI-driven gains in megacap tech, and resilient consumer spending amid cooling but sticky inflation at 3.3% YoY in March per latest CPI data. Federal funds rate holds steady at 3.5%-3.75% following the April FOMC meeting, with markets pricing in potential 25-basis-point cuts by mid-year if labor data softens further from current low jobless claims around 219,000. Traders eye April CPI release on May 12 and June 16-17 FOMC for policy signals, alongside nonfarm payrolls, as volatility risks linger from geopolitical tensions; consensus analyst targets point to 7,500-7,600 by year-end, implying modest 4-6% upside to June close.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Open Market Committee held the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%-3.75% in its April 28-29 meeting, marking the third consecutive pause amid hotter-than-expected inflation data, with March CPI surging to 3.3% year-over-year and PCE at 3.5%—driven partly by energy prices. This reflects trader consensus pricing limited near-term cuts, as futures imply rates around 3.6% through mid-2026, aligning with the March dot plot's median end-2026 projection of 3.4%. Solid labor markets, evidenced by March unemployment at 4.3%, support caution against easing. Key catalysts include May 12 CPI release and the June 16-17 FOMC, where persistent inflation above 2% could delay cuts toward lower levels before 2027.

The 98.8% market-implied probability for 74°F or higher reflects traders' confidence in this trajectory, backed by real-money consensus on observational data. Realistic challenges include a stalled frontal boundary introducing clouds or isolated thunderstorms, with fresh model runs and NWS updates expected daily through Sunday.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 500-519 tweets from April 24 to May 1, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 24 12:00 PM ET to May 1, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:

markets.question: Will Trump say "Obamacare" at The Villages on May 1?
markets.description:
Donald Trump is scheduled to visit The Villages on May 1, 2026, 3PM ET (https://www.dailycommercial.com/story/news/local/2026/04/28/president-trump-set-to-visit-the-villages-in-florida-on-may-1-2026/89835008007/).

If clips of old interviews or prerecorded videos are aired where Trump is speaking, those clips will count toward this market's resolution.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g., joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however, "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market is explicitly about Trump's visit to The Villages scheduled for May 1, 2026, 3PM ET. Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of the named, scheduled event will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

markets.question: ICC Cricket World Cup League Two: Nepal vs United Arab Emirates

  This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,200 (HIGH) in December?
markets.description:

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD trades around 1.174 following the European Central Bank's April 30 decision to hold key interest rates unchanged amid Eurozone annual inflation rising to 3.0% in April's flash estimate, up from 2.6% in March. The Federal Reserve similarly maintained the federal funds rate at 3.5%-3.75% on April 29, despite notable internal dissent and U.S. March CPI at 3.3%, signaling persistent inflationary pressures curbing near-term easing expectations. Trader consensus reflects limited ECB-Fed policy divergence through 2026, with analyst projections clustering near 1.16-1.19 amid subdued Eurozone growth. Key catalysts include ECB's May 20 Governing Council review, FOMC's June 16-17 meeting, and May CPI data, which could shift rate path pricing and exchange rate volatility.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Open Market Committee held the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%-3.75% in its April 28-29 meeting, marking the third consecutive pause amid hotter-than-expected inflation data, with March CPI surging to 3.3% year-over-year and PCE at 3.5%—driven partly by energy prices. This reflects trader consensus pricing limited near-term cuts, as futures imply rates around 3.6% through mid-2026, aligning with the March dot plot's median end-2026 projection of 3.4%. Solid labor markets, evidenced by March unemployment at 4.3%, support caution against easing. Key catalysts include May 12 CPI release and the June 16-17 FOMC, where persistent inflation above 2% could delay cuts toward lower levels before 2027.

If a player's team is eliminated, and they are not in the goal lead, the market for that player may resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA Champions League. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kylian Mbappé leads the UEFA Champions League top scorer race with 15 goals in 11 matches for Real Madrid, ahead of Harry Kane's 13 in 12 for Bayern Munich, fueling trader consensus at 57% and 43.5% implied probabilities in this closely contested market. Semi-final first-leg results three days ago preserved Mbappé's two-goal edge without significant advances from challengers, highlighting his elite 1.36 goals-per-game rate versus Kane's 1.08 amid both teams' deep knockout runs. Real Madrid's attacking firepower and Mbappé's form give him a slight advantage, while Kane's clinical finishing and Bayern's schedule keep him viable. Others like Julián Álvarez (10 goals) and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia trail distantly with limited fixtures left.

markets.question: Elon Bull Run Parlay

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors "No" at 88% implied probability for the Elon Bull Run Parlay, reflecting skepticism that all three 2026 milestones—Elon Musk hitting $1 trillion net worth per Bloomberg Billionaires Index, announcing another child, and SpaceX achieving 9+ Starship launches reaching 100 km altitude—will align. Musk's fortune hovers around $800 billion amid Tesla shares trading flat near $380 post-Q1 earnings, insufficient for the required surge despite SpaceX's April 7 Flight 12 success and IPO rumors targeting $1.5-2 trillion valuation in June. No new baby confirmations have emerged, and Starship's pace (just one qualifier YTD) underscores cadence risks. Upcoming Flight 13 in early May and potential SpaceX listing could shift sentiment, but parlay math demands flawless execution across personal, financial, and technical fronts.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will US withdraw from NATO by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's administration escalated NATO withdrawal rhetoric in April 2026 after allies declined support for US military actions against Iran, with White House Press Secretary Caroline Leavitt confirming on April 9 that such discussions are underway ahead of talks with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte. Trader consensus prices low probabilities—around 11% for exit before 2027—due to Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act, which bars unilateral presidential withdrawal without congressional approval via two-thirds Senate consent or legislation. Bipartisan opposition, including from Republican senators, reinforces barriers, despite prior steps like the January Pentagon pullout of 200 officers from NATO structures. Upcoming Rutte-Trump meetings and potential congressional pushback could further shape alliance dynamics.
